data.title: Tesla earnings call in focus as prediction markets eye Optimus, SpaceX, and tariffs
data.description:
All eyes are on Tesla (TSLA) ahead of its earnings release after the closing bell on Wednesday, with investors looking for clarity following a 14.1% decline in the stock so far in 2026. The upcoming earnings call is expected to be a key catalyst, offering insight into both near-term performance and longer-term strategic priorities.

According to prediction market Kalshi, traders are assigning high probabilities to several themes being discussed on the call. Among the most anticipated topics are Tesla’s Optimus humanoid robot initiative, developments tied to SpaceX and xAI, along with the potential impact of tariffs on the company’s cost structure and global operations.

Outlined below is what traders on Kalshi are forecasting as to what Tesla will say in its upcoming earnings call:

  • Optimus — 99% chance.
  • FSD / Full Self Driving — 98% chance.
  • Delivery — 95% chance.
  • Battery — 94% chance.
  • Semi — 90% chance.
  • Terafab — 89% chance.
  • Regulatory / Regulation — 87% chance.
  • China — 84% chance.
  • SpaceX / xAI — 84% chance. 
  • Tariff — 81% chance. 
  • Roadster — 72% chance.
  • Competition — 61% chance. 
  • Cybertruck — 59% chance. 
  • Leader / Leadership — 52% chance. 
  • Dojo / Dojo3 — 47% chance.
  • Supercharger / Super Charger — 45% chance. 
  • Gigafactory — 39% chance. 
  • Iran — 25% chance. 
  • Grok 5 — 17% chance.
  • Trump — 15% chance.

data.title: Block partners with Uber to launch Cash App Pay into Uber, Uber Eats in U.S.
data.description:
Block (XYZ) has partnered with Uber Technologies (UBER) to introduce Cash App Pay to Uber and Uber Eats in the country.

Additionally, the partnership is expected to bring the Block unit Square's native Uber Eats integration to international markets, including Canada, Australia, the U.K., Ireland, France, and Spain.

Last year, Uber partnered with Block's Buy Now, Pay Later solution, Afterpay, in Australia to offer greater flexibility for the Australian customers shopping on Uber and Uber Eats.

XYZ shares were 1.38% higher at $72.74 during pre-market trading on Wednesday, while UBER was up 1.07% to $78.09.

data.title: Anheuser-Busch invests in U.S. manufacturing as it retains its strong market share lead
data.description:
Anheuser-Busch (BUD) announced on Wednesday that it is expanding its commitment to creating and sustaining American manufacturing jobs by increasing its investment in its U.S. operations to $600M across 2025 and 2026. The beer giant said the investment builds on its 165-year legacy of investing in people, breweries, and communities.

"By strengthening our manufacturing operations, we are creating sustainable careers – not just jobs – and investing in the people who are vital to our success," highlighted CEO Brendan Whitworth.

Notably, Anheuser-Busch (BUD) is still the beer market share leader in the U.S., with Michelob Ultra (#1), Bud Light (#3), Budweiser (#7), and Busch Light (#8) all in the top ten based on the most recent Nielsen year-to-date data.

Shares of BUD are up more than 13% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: SunPower raises $41M through convertible notes offering
data.description:
• SunPower (SPWR) on Wednesday said it raised $41M through the sale of convertible senior secured notes.
  • The notes mature on May 1, 2029, carry a 10% coupon, and include a 45% conversion premium to the company’s prior closing share price of $1.13, implying a conversion price of $1.6385.
  • Financing is expected to close this week. SunPower said proceeds will be used for working capital and to repay $18.75M of debt.
  • The company also said certain holders agreed to exchange $21.25M of existing 7% notes for stock, while former owners of Sunder converted $10M of acquisition-related debt into the new notes.
  • SunPower said the transactions would reduce total debt by about $40M.
  • Shares down roughly 10% in premarket trading

data.title: BrightSpire raised to Buy at BTIG on earnings, portfolio growth outlook
data.description:
BTIG upgraded BrightSpire Capital (BRSP) to Buy from Neutral after gaining confidence that the commercial mortgage REIT will be able to achieve both portfolio and earnings growth in 2026.

BrightSpire (BRSP) stock gained 2.2% in Wednesday premarket trading.

Analyst Thomas Catherwood had been sitting on the sidelines as earnings growth “proved elusive” in 2025.

Citing the company's Q4 22025 origination activity, where its loan portfolio grew 13.3% from the prior quarter, low-levered balance sheet of 2.3x debt-to-equity at year end, and path to unlocking “stuck” capital, BTIG now expects its loan book to grow 30% more in 2026 to ~$3.5B.

"We project this increase in portfolio size to drive an earnings inflection with DE (distributable earnings) of $0.67 in full-year 2026 and $0.74 in 2027 (4.7% and 10.4% annual growth, respectively)," Catherwood wrote in a note to clients.

"BRSP is well on its way to achieving our two key drivers for 2026 outperformance – net portfolio growth and sustainable earnings growth," he wrote.

The Buy rating aligns with the average Wall Street rating.

data.title: Medicare delays GLP-1 coverage plan; interim program extended
data.description:
The Trump administration has extended a short-term program that offers Medicare coverage for weight loss drugs such as Novo’s (NVO) Wegovy and Lilly’s (LLY) Zepbound after leading health insurers balked at participating in a long-term payment model.

According to the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services ((CMS)), the interim coverage program that is scheduled to start in July will now run until the end of 2027, compared to its previous plans to end by the end of this year.

As part of the program, the agency will directly pay for the weight loss medications rather than adding them to Medicare plans and forcing health insurers to cover the expenses.

Medicare extended the interim program “after listening to stakeholder feedback,” The Wall Street Journal reported, quoting a spokeswoman from CMS. The decision will “allow data collection that will support a more effective potential implementation” of the longer-term model, she added.

Previously, CVS Health (CVS) announced its intention not to participate in Medicare’s long-term GLP-1 coverage model known as BALANCE. UnitedHealth (UNH), the largest Medicare Advantage insurer, raised concerns about its structure.

With the participation of health insurers representing at least 80% of Medicare Part D members requiring the implementation of BALANCE, the companies were given a deadline until April 20 to inform CMS of their decisions.

The agency said it still plans to roll out the longer-term model in Medicaid, and it was seeking applications from states until the end of July.

data.title: Broadcom expands collaboration with Google Cloud
data.description:
• Broadcom (AVGO) on Wednesday announced an expanded collaboration with Google Cloud (GOOG) on their new Cloud Network Insights service enabled by AppNeta by Broadcom.
  • AVGO +1.44% premarket to $407.99.
  • Cloud Network Insights provides end-to-end network observability to help organizations manage application and network experience, detect issues, and diagnose root cause across complex, multi-cloud, hybrid, and agentic environments, Broadcom said.
  • The company added that Cloud Network Insights benefits would include end-to-end network path visibility to monitor critical network metrics, agent and application experience and performance monitoring across on-premises, cloud, and SaaS environments in one place, and more.
  • “By bringing AppNeta’s comprehensive observability technologies into our Cloud Network Insights product, we are equipping customers with the end-to-end insights they need to proactively optimize agent, application, and network experiences and rapidly pinpoint network bottlenecks," said Rob Enns, VP and General Manager of Cloud Networking, Google Cloud.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Microsoft to face $2.8B UK lawsuit over cloud computing licenses
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) must face a mass lawsuit in the U.K. alleging the company overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software ​on cloud computing services provided by Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), and Alibaba (BABA), according to a ruling by a London tribunal.

Competition lawyer Maria Luisa Stasi is bringing the case on behalf of about 60,000 businesses that run Windows Server on competing cloud platforms. Her lawyers have ​previously said the claim was worth up to 2.1B pounds (about $2.8B), according to a report by Reuters.

Last year, they argued at a hearing that the businesses were overcharged ⁠because Microsoft charges higher wholesale prices for Windows Server than for users ​of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ​than Amazon's Amazon Web Services, or AWS, or Google Cloud, the report noted. Microsoft had said Stasi's case failed to set out a workable method for calculating any alleged losses and should be thrown out.

The U.K.'s Competition Appeal Tribunal said in its ruling that Microsoft's primary position in respect of the application was that it should be refused entirely. 

Maria Luisa Stasi had applied to be appointed as the proposed class representative, or PCR. There were several issues in dispute, including whether the PCR provided an adequate blueprint to trial said to pass the Pro-Sys Test. 

In this regard, the tribunal concluded that, "however novel the PCR's theory of harm underlying her claim may be, it posited losses on a class-wide basis which arose straightforwardly from the PCMs [ proposed class members] having faced an overcharge as a result of Microsoft's licensing practices, thereby meeting the nub of the inquiry under the Pro-Sys Test."

"The Tribunal thereby certified the claim on the terms sought by the PCR," said the tribunal in the ruling on Tuesday.

Microsoft did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

A Microsoft spokesperson ​told Reuters that they planned to appeal against the decision. "We also dispute the underlying allegations by the ‌class ⁠representative (Stasi), and today's decision makes no final determination on those claims," said the spokesperson.

Stasi said in a statement that the ruling was "an important moment for the thousands of organizations impacted by Microsoft's conduct," according to the report.

At last year's hearing, Microsoft noted that ​its vertically integrated ​business model — using ⁠Windows Server as an input for Azure while also licensing it to rivals — can benefit competition, the report added.

In July 2025, U.K.'s Competition and Markets Authority said Microsoft and AWS were hurting competition in the cloud computing industry and called for a review into their market dominance under the country’s new tech rules. Microsoft said at that time that "the CMA Panel’s most recent publication misses the mark again, ignoring that the cloud market has never been so dynamic and competitive, with record investment, and rapid, AI-driven changes. Its recommendations fail to cover Google, one of the fastest-growing cloud market participants."

data.title: Auri appoints Bon Halder as interim CEO
data.description:
• Auri (AURI) announced on Wednesday the appointment of Bon Halder as interim chief executive officer.
  • As interim CEO, Halder is expected to strengthen AURI's operational structure, align leadership with execution-focused priorities, and support the company's broader strategy of maximizing shareholder value through disciplined growth in the energy sector, the company said. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vulcan Value Partners Large Cap Portfolio adds SAP, NOW; exits CSGP, DEO among Q1 moves
data.description:
Vulcan Value Partners Large Cap Portfolio returned -14.1% for the quarter, underperforming the Russell 1000 Value Index and the S&P 500 Index.

The fund, in its quarterly letter, said that there were no material contributors to performance. On the other hand, there were six material detractors to performance, including Salesforce (CRM), Ryan Specialty (RYAN), Ares Management (ARES), TPG (TPG), SAP SE (SAP), and Microsoft (MSFT).

In the first quarter, the fund initiated two new positions: SAP SE (SAP) and ServiceNow (NOW).

On the other hand, the fund exited six positions during the quarter, including CoStar Group (CSGP), Diageo (DEO), Bureau Veritas SA (BVRDF), Stanley Black & Decker (SWK), Crown Holdings (CCK), and Qorvo (QRVO).

Source: Q1 fund letter
